root_text.ud3.8:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.
root_text.ud3.8:1.3: Tena kho pana samayena sambahulānaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ pacchābhattaṁ piṇḍapātapaṭikkantānaṁ karerimaṇḍalamāḷe sannisinnānaṁ sannipatitānaṁ ayamantarākathā udapādi:
root_text.ud3.8:2.1: “Piṇḍapātiko, āvuso, bhikkhu piṇḍāya caranto labhati kālena kālaṁ manāpike cakkhunā rūpe passituṁ, labhati kālena kālaṁ manāpike sotena sadde sotuṁ, labhati kālena kālaṁ manāpike ghānena gandhe ghāyituṁ, labhati kālena kālaṁ manāpike jivhāya rase sāyituṁ, labhati kālena kālaṁ manāpike kāyena phoṭṭhabbe phusituṁ.
root_text.ud3.8:2.2: Piṇḍapātiko, āvuso, bhikkhu sakkato garukato mānito pūjito apacito piṇḍāya carati.
root_text.ud3.8:2.4: Mayampi lacchāma kālena kālaṁ manāpike cakkhunā rūpe passituṁ, mayampi lacchāma kālena kālaṁ manāpike sotena sadde sotuṁ, mayampi lacchāma kālena kālaṁ manāpike ghānena gandhe ghāyituṁ, mayampi lacchāma kālena kālaṁ manāpike jivhāya rase sāyituṁ, mayampi lacchāma kālena kālaṁ manāpike kāyena phoṭṭhabbe phusituṁ;
root_text.ud3.8:2.5: mayampi sakkatā garukatā mānitā pūjitā apacitā piṇḍāya carissāmā”ti.
root_text.ud3.8:2.6: Ayañcarahi tesaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ antarākathā hoti vippakatā.
root_text.ud3.8:3.1: Atha kho bhagavā sāyanhasamayaṁ paṭisallānā vuṭṭhito yena karerimaṇḍalamāḷo tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā paññatte āsane nisīdi.
root_text.ud3.8:3.3: “kāya nuttha, bhikkhave, etarahi kathāya sannisinnā, kā ca pana vo antarākathā vippakatā”ti?
root_text.ud3.8:4.1: “Idha, bhante, amhākaṁ pacchābhattaṁ piṇḍapātapaṭikkantānaṁ karerimaṇḍalamāḷe sannisinnānaṁ sannipatitānaṁ ayamantarākathā udapādi:
root_text.ud3.8:5.1: ‘Piṇḍapātiko, āvuso, bhikkhu piṇḍāya caranto labhati kālena kālaṁ manāpike cakkhunā rūpe passituṁ, labhati kālena kālaṁ manāpike sotena sadde sotuṁ, labhati kālena kālaṁ manāpike ghānena gandhe ghāyituṁ, labhati kālena kālaṁ manāpike jivhāya rase sāyituṁ, labhati kālena kālaṁ manāpike kāyena phoṭṭhabbe phusituṁ.
root_text.ud3.8:5.2: Piṇḍapātiko, āvuso, bhikkhu sakkato garukato mānito pūjito apacito piṇḍāya carati.
root_text.ud3.8:5.4: Mayampi lacchāma kālena kālaṁ manāpike cakkhunā rūpe passituṁ …pe…
root_text.ud3.8:5.6: Mayampi sakkatā garukatā mānitā pūjitā apacitā piṇḍāya carissāmā’ti.
root_text.ud3.8:5.7: Ayaṁ kho no, bhante, antarākathā vippakatā, atha bhagavā anuppatto”ti.
root_text.ud3.8:6.1: “Na khvetaṁ, bhikkhave, tumhākaṁ patirūpaṁ kulaputtānaṁ saddhā agārasmā anagāriyaṁ pabbajitānaṁ yaṁ tumhe evarūpiṁ kathaṁ katheyyātha.
root_text.ud3.8:6.3: dhammī vā kathā ariyo vā tuṇhībhāvo”ti.
root_text.ud3.8:7.1: Atha kho bhagavā etamatthaṁ viditvā tāyaṁ velāyaṁ imaṁ udānaṁ udānesi:

translation_text.ud3.8:1.2: At one time the Buddha was staying near Sāvatthī in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
translation_text.ud3.8:1.3: Now at that time, after the meal, on return from almsround, several mendicants sat together in the pavilion by the kareri tree and this discussion came up among them:
translation_text.ud3.8:2.1: “Reverends, when a mendicant who eats only almsfood is wandering for alms, from time to time they get to see pleasing sights, hear pleasing sounds, smell pleasing smells, taste pleasing tastes, and encounter pleasing touches.
translation_text.ud3.8:2.2: They wander for alms being honored, respected, revered, venerated, and esteemed.
translation_text.ud3.8:2.3: Come, we too should eat only almsfood.
translation_text.ud3.8:2.4: From time to time we too will get to see pleasing sights, hear pleasing sounds, smell pleasing smells, taste pleasing tastes, and encounter pleasing touches.
translation_text.ud3.8:2.5: We too shall wander for alms being honored, respected, revered, venerated, and esteemed.”
translation_text.ud3.8:2.6: At that point the conversation among those mendicants was left unfinished.
translation_text.ud3.8:3.1: Then in the late afternoon, the Buddha came out of retreat and went to the pavilion by the kareri tree, where he sat on the seat spread out
translation_text.ud3.8:3.3: “Mendicants, what were you sitting talking about just now? What conversation was left unfinished?”
translation_text.ud3.8:4.1: So the mendicants told him what they had been talking about. The Buddha said,
translation_text.ud3.8:6.1: “Mendicants, it is not appropriate for you gentlemen who have gone forth out of faith from the lay life to homelessness to talk about such things.
translation_text.ud3.8:6.2: When you’re sitting together you should do one of two things:
translation_text.ud3.8:6.3: discuss the teachings or keep noble silence.”
translation_text.ud3.8:7.1: Then, understanding this matter, on that occasion the Buddha expressed this heartfelt sentiment:
translation_text.ud3.8:8.3: the unaffected one is envied by even the gods,
translation_text.ud3.8:8.4: but not if they’re after popularity and reputation.”
